title: V3 Cart-First Checkout
purpose: Teach the agent to use the current V3 canonical server cart flow without treating local CLI state as business truth.
when_to_use: The user already chose a catalog item, variant, and offer.
The agent needs to add a catalog item to a server cart before checkout.
The agent wants to understand how service-backed cart items create or resume Service Execution.

must_not_need: catalog search support from this CLI

commands.intent: add the first line to the canonical server cart
commands.success_signal: JSON includes cart_id and cart_item_id; service-backed lines also include service_execution_id and top-level next_actions

commands.intent: recover the next step for the current cart
commands.success_signal: JSON includes next_actions derived from the canonical server cart and Service Execution read model

commands.intent: inspect the current canonical server cart before checkout
commands.success_signal: server cart lines, checkout readiness, and service refs are printed

commands.intent: soft-remove one active line from the canonical server cart
commands.success_signal: the line is removed from the server cart; quote-locked or checkout-bound lines return a conflict
the server cart is abandoned, active lines are soft-removed, and local handles are cleared

commands.intent: create checkout from a server cart that is ready for checkout
commands.success_signal: the CLI either renders checkout_qr or emits an interaction request for missing contact data

commands.intent: create quote lock and checkout for a service-backed cart item
commands.success_signal: the quote lock is bound to the original server cart item when present; JSON includes checkout_id and display_token

commands.intent: read the next Service Execution action
commands.success_signal: JSON includes next_actions such as invoke capability, select result item, or checkout

agent_rules: The current V3 CLI uses server cart as the business source of truth. Local ~/.itpay-v3/cart.json only caches last ids and explicit local drafts.
Prefer top-level next_actions and itpay next/cart next/services next over hardcoded service-specific flows.
Use cart add/show/remove/clear before checkout. Remove and clear are server-side soft state changes, not hard deletes.
For service-backed items, use service_execution_id from cart add/show as the canonical handle for services invoke/action/checkout.
Do not call buy --cart on a service-backed cart item until the required service action and quote lock are complete.
Do not remove quote-locked or checkout-bound cart lines. Continue checkout/payment or use refund/cancel owner flow after payment.
If required contact data is missing, prefer --require-contact so the CLI can emit a structured interaction request.
Do not invent buyer email or phone values; let the human provide them when required.
The happy-path buy flow creates cart and checkout, then renders checkout_qr. It should not create a payment intent first.
forbidden: Do not treat local cart files as durable business truth.
Do not create duplicate checkout commands if the first handoff is still usable.
Do not treat QR display as proof of payment.

next_docs.condition: Need host rendering details after checkout is created
Need order or refund follow-up after payment
